articles.title: Scientists discovered a secret deal between a plant and beetles
articles.title_tr: Scientists discovered a secret deal between a plant and beetles
articles.summary: A study from Kobe University has uncovered a surprising partnership between Japanese red elder plants and Heterhelus beetles. The beetles pollinate the flowers but also lay eggs inside the developing fruit. The plant responds by dropping many of those fruits, yet the larvae survive by escaping into the soil. The discovery suggests that fruit drop is not punishment but a compromise that keeps the plant–insect relationship stable.

articles.title: Scientists discover a universal temperature curve that governs all life
articles.title_tr: Scientists discover a universal temperature curve that governs all life
articles.summary: Researchers have uncovered a universal pattern showing how temperature affects life on Earth. Across thousands of species—from microbes to reptiles—performance rises gradually with warming until an optimal temperature is reached, after which it drops sharply. Although each species has its own preferred temperature range, they all follow the same underlying curve. This surprising constraint suggests evolution may have limited room to help species cope with rapid climate warming.

articles.title: A black hole and neutron star just collided in a strange oval orbit
articles.title_tr: A black hole and neutron star just collided in a strange oval orbit
articles.summary: Scientists analyzing a gravitational-wave signal have discovered that a neutron star and black hole spiraled together on an oval-shaped orbit just before merging. This unusual motion, detected in the event GW200105, contradicts the long-held expectation that such pairs settle into nearly perfect circles before collision. The eccentric orbit suggests the system likely formed in a chaotic stellar environment with strong gravitational interactions.

articles.title: Scientists discover hedgehogs can hear ultrasound and it could save them from cars
articles.title_tr: Scientists discover hedgehogs can hear ultrasound and it could save them from cars
articles.summary: Researchers have discovered that hedgehogs can hear ultrasound, a surprising ability that could help protect them from cars. Since road traffic kills large numbers of hedgehogs, scientists believe ultrasonic repellents might be used to steer them away from danger. The animals’ ears appear specially adapted for detecting high-frequency sounds. If the idea works, cars could one day emit signals that warn hedgehogs before it’s too late.
